_meta.description: App is in foreground. User briefly switches to launcher (simulating a notification tap or link open in browser), then a deep link brings them back. onDeepLinking fires with FOUND via the onResume/onNewIntent path.
_meta.status: CAPTURED — baselines reflect real observed payloads
_meta.important_note: A deep link sent directly to a foregrounded app via adb onNewIntent (without any pause/resume cycle) does NOT trigger onDeepLinking. The app must go through at least a brief onPause before the SDK processes the new deep link. The test procedure uses a launcher switch to simulate this real-world pattern.

preconditions: SDK fully started — startSDK result: SUCCESS confirmed in logs
App is in the foreground (no home press yet)

android: confirm app is in foreground: adb shell dumpsys activity activities | grep mResumedActivity | grep appsflyer
briefly switch to launcher: adb shell am start -a android.intent.action.MAIN -c android.intent.category.HOME

trigger deep link (brings app back to foreground): adb shell am start -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d "afexample://deeplink?deep_link_value=qa_deeplink_fg&af_sub1=foreground_test&pid=testmedia&c=deeplink_test"
ios: sleep 12 (wait for SDK start + onInstallConversionData with is_first_launch=true)
confirm app is in foreground (PID 2473 alive)
briefly switch to Settings to trigger onPause: xcrun simctl launch E112EA95-9BAE-4BE1-921C-FCCC39CF5F31 com.apple.Preferences
xcrun simctl spawn E112EA95-9BAE-4BE1-921C-FCCC39CF5F31 log show --predicate 'process == "Runner"' --last 30s --style compact | grep AF_QA
procedure.why_launcher_switch: The AppsFlyer SDK processes deep links on onResume. When onNewIntent fires without a preceding onPause, the SDK does not re-trigger the deep link callback. The brief launcher switch causes onPause → (deep link intent) → onResume, which is the standard real-world deep link path.

onInstallConversionData_second_fire.note: Same as background scenario — onInstallConversionData fires again with is_first_launch=false on app resume.

onAppOpenAttribution.note: Does not fire for custom URI scheme (non-OneLink) deep links.

http_request.description: A LAUNCH event is sent when the app resumes via deep link.

background_vs_foreground_differences.deepLinkValue: bg=qa_deeplink_bg, fg=qa_deeplink_fg — use this to confirm which scenario ran
background_vs_foreground_differences.onInstallConversionData: Both scenarios fire a second onInstallConversionData with is_first_launch=false
background_vs_foreground_differences.onAppOpenAttribution: Neither fires for custom URI scheme — would need OneLink URL
background_vs_foreground_differences.sdk_behavior: Both are functionally identical at the SDK level — both go through onPause → onNewIntent → onResume → SDK processes intent

ios_captured.captured_from: iOS simulator E112EA95-9BAE-4BE1-921C-FCCC39CF5F31 iPhone 17, fresh install 2026-03-16
ios_captured.install_conversion_first_fire_log: [AF_QA][CALLBACK][onInstallConversionData] received: {status: success, payload: {is_first_launch: true, install_time: 2026-03-16 11:56:46.220, af_message: organic install, af_status: Organic}} (2026-03-16 13:56:50)
ios_captured.onDeepLinking_log: 2026-03-16 13:57:30.276 Df Runner[2473:4fcfd0] (Flutter) flutter: [AF_QA][CALLBACK][onDeepLinking] received: status=Status.FOUND deepLinkValue=qa_deeplink_fg error=null
ios_captured.onInstallConversionData_second_fire_log: 2026-03-16 13:57:31.257 Df Runner[2473:4fcfd0] (Flutter) flutter: [AF_QA][CALLBACK][onInstallConversionData] received: {status: success, payload: {is_first_launch: false, install_time: 2026-03-16 11:56:46.220, af_message: organic install, af_status: Organic}}
ios_captured.http_log_sample: 2026-03-16 13:57:30.505 Df Runner[2473:4fd88a] [com.apple.CFNetwork:Summary] Task <47FF50C9-33FD-4466-B18D-787EA9D7DB5A>.<18> summary for task success {transaction_duration_ms=232, response_status=200, ...}
notes: App was briefly backgrounded via Settings launch (com.apple.Preferences) before deep link trigger — this is required to trigger the onPause/onResume cycle on iOS
onDeepLinking fired 981ms before onInstallConversionData second fire
HTTP 200 response observed at same timestamp as onDeepLinking callback (13:57:30)
Initial onDeepLinking fires with Status.NOT_FOUND on first launch (before any deep link) — this is expected iOS SDK behavior
onAppOpenAttribution did not fire — confirmed not expected for custom URI scheme
iOS behavior matches Android: both require a brief background cycle (onPause) before the SDK processes the deep link on resume
